----- Original Message ----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Tuesday, March 6, 2007 6:26:29 AM
Subject: FW: For Mr. Jeffrey Epstein-2 dates-Please specify which date you will be attending/Black Book


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Lesley Groff
Executive Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein
 
 




From: Peggy Siegal [mailto:peggy@peggysiegal.com] 
Sent: Monday, March 05, 2007 6:00 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: For Mr. Jeffrey Epstein-2 dates-Please specify which date you will be attending/Black Book


 
SONY PICTURES CLASSICS
MICHAEL BARKER   TOM BERNARD   
CO-PRESIDENT                     CO-PRESIDENT
CORDIALLY INVITE YOU TO
A PRIVATE SCREENING OF
BLACK BOOK
CARICE VAN HOUTEN   SEBASTIAN KOCH   THOM HOFFMAN
DIRECTED BY PAUL VERHOEVEN
WRITTEN BY GERARD SOETEMAN & PAUL VERHOEVEN   
WEDNESDAY, MARCH 21ST 
OR
TUESDAY, MARCH 27TH 
6:30 P.M. DOLBY SCREENING ROOM
  1350 AVENUE OF THE AMERICAS - ON 55TH STREET
9:00 P.M. OSTERIA DEL CIRCO
120 WEST 55TH STREET
PEGGY@PEGGYSIEGAL.COM OR 212-935-6700
THIS INVITATION IS NON-TRANSFERABLE
A relentlessly gripping thriller about the Dutch underground set in the fall of 1944, the film marks director Paul Verhoeven’s (“Basic Instinct”) return to his native Netherlands revisiting the action filled World War II subject matter of his 1977 Dutch drama “Soldier of Orange.”  
BLACK BOOK is based on true events that span nearly a year around Rachel Stein (Carice van Houten) a young, pretty Jewish woman who falls for a high-ranking Gestapo officer (Sebastian Koch – “The Lives of Others”) while seeking revenge for her family's murders. The multi-layered characters find themselves embroiled in a spider’s web of intrigue, treachery and betrayal.  
BLACK BOOK is a highly stylized film suffused with the intense paranoia Verhoeven evokes so well, where friend and enemy blur together into an indistinguishable line. 

 
This email was sent to lesley@nysgllc.com, by peggy@peggysiegal.com
